compiled_state.title: Implement figma-node-mapper.py: Figma JSON tree to spatial-layout.json mapping
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## What
Implement plugins/dso/scripts/figma-node-mapper.py that reads a Figma JSON node tree from stdin, maps it to the spatial-layout.json schema with depth-limited traversal and component identity matching, and outputs valid spatial-layout.json to stdout.

## testing_mode: RED
New source file implementing new behavior. Depends on RED test task 3366-8ca5.

## Interface
Input: stdin (raw Figma JSON from figma-api-fetch.sh) + optional --depth-limit <int> (default 20) + optional --original-manifest <path> (for identity matching)
Output: valid spatial-layout.json to stdout

## Implementation Steps
1. Parse Figma JSON from stdin using json.loads
2. Implement depth-limited recursive traversal: map_nodes(node, depth=0, depth_limit=20) — returns None when depth exceeds limit
3. Node type mapping:
   - FRAME / GROUP → type='Section', spatial_hint from absoluteBoundingBox
   - TEXT → type='TextNode', text from characters, spatial_hint from absoluteBoundingBox
   - RECTANGLE / VECTOR → type='Shape', props.fills/strokes from fills/strokes
   - COMPONENT → type='Component', componentId preserved
   - INSTANCE → type='Instance', componentId from componentSetId or name
   - Unknown → type='Unknown', preserve id/name
4. Identity matching (identity_match function, also exported for S3):
   - Primary: match Figma node name to original manifest component id
   - Fallback: structural position (index in parent's children array)
   - Unmatched new Figma node → flag unmatched_new=True
   - Unmatched original component → flag unmatched_original=True (returned separately)
5. Schema validation: check required fields (storyId, designId, layout, components) and component fields (id, type, tag)
6. Output: json.dumps(spatial_layout, indent=2)

## Files to Create
- plugins/dso/scripts/figma-node-mapper.py

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] plugins/dso/scripts/figma-node-mapper.py exists
  Verify: test -f plugins/dso/scripts/figma-node-mapper.py
- [ ] pytest tests/unit/scripts/test_figma_node_mapper.py exits 0 (all NM-1 through NM-11 pass)
  Verify: poetry run pytest tests/unit/scripts/test_figma_node_mapper.py --tb=short -q
- [ ] Script reads from stdin: echo '{...}' | python3 plugins/dso/scripts/figma-node-mapper.py exits 0
  Verify: echo '{"document":{"children":[]},"name":"test"}' | python3 plugins/dso/scripts/figma-node-mapper.py
- [ ] Does not use jq or subprocess to call jq
  Verify: ! grep -q 'jq' plugins/dso/scripts/figma-node-mapper.py
- [ ] identity_match function is importable as a module (for use by S3 merge script)
  Verify: python3 -c 'from figma_node_mapper import identity_match' 2>/dev/null || python3 -c 'import sys; sys.path.insert(0,"plugins/dso/scripts"); from figma_node_mapper import identity_match'
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh exits 0
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh

comments.body:
AC amendment: Before implementing identity_match(), create a brief contract note in plugins/dso/docs/contracts/figma-node-mapper-output.md documenting the identity_match() interface: inputs (figma_nodes list, original_manifest dict), outputs (matched_map dict, unmatched_new list, unmatched_original list), and matching priority (name→id primary, structural position fallback). This ensures NM-7..10 and the implementation share a stable interface contract.

AC amendment: Schema validation in NM-11 must reference plugins/dso/docs/contracts/spatial-layout.schema.json (created by ed14-e238). Use jsonschema.validate() or equivalent.
comments.author: Test
